scoring_criteria.demand: Strength of market demand signals from community, enterprise, and research (0-100)
scoring_criteria.competition: Level of existing competition addressing this gap (0=none, 100=fully addressed)
scoring_criteria.feasibility: Technical and business feasibility of building a solution (0-100)

ranked_gaps.title: End-to-End Domain Data Pipeline (Raw Docs to Training Data)
ranked_gaps.rationale: Demand is the highest among all gaps - #1 cited cause of enterprise fine-tuning failure. Competition is low: easy-dataset and Augmentoolkit exist as early OSS tools, Meta's synthetic-data-kit is new, but no integrated platform solution exists. Highly feasible with existing LLM APIs for extraction + established parsing libraries. This is the most actionable high-impact gap.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Unified platform that ingests raw domain documents (PDFs, contracts, medical records, code), extracts structured knowledge, generates instruction-following pairs, validates quality, and outputs ready-to-train datasets in standard formats.
ranked_gaps.target_market: Enterprise ML teams, domain-specific AI startups, consulting firms

ranked_gaps.title: Domain-Specific Evaluation Framework
ranked_gaps.rationale: Evaluation is universally cited as a critical gap. LLM eval platforms exist (Langfuse, Arize, etc.) but none focus on domain-specific evaluation for fine-tuned SLMs with pre-built evaluation suites for healthcare, legal, finance. Medium competition from general eval tools but very low from domain-specialized ones. Feasible but requires domain expertise investment.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Domain-specific evaluation platform with pre-built test suites for healthcare (clinical accuracy, ICD codes, HIPAA), legal (citation accuracy, clause extraction), finance (numeric precision, regulatory compliance). Includes LLM-as-Judge with domain-trained judge models, regression testing for catastrophic forgetting, and production monitoring.
ranked_gaps.target_market: Enterprise teams in regulated industries, AI safety/compliance teams

ranked_gaps.title: Synthetic Domain Data Generation Platform
ranked_gaps.rationale: High demand for generating domain-specific training data, especially for data-scarce domains. Growing competition from Distilabel, Curator, Meta synthetic-data-kit, but none offer a domain-aware platform (ontology-guided, quality-validated). Highly feasible using existing LLM APIs as teacher models.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Platform that takes domain knowledge base (documents, ontologies, examples) and generates high-quality synthetic training data using teacher LLMs, with quality validation, deduplication, and diversity scoring. Includes domain-specific templates for healthcare, legal, finance, and code.
ranked_gaps.target_market: ML teams in data-scarce domains, startups building vertical AI products

ranked_gaps.title: Edge/On-Device SLM Fine-Tuning and Deployment Platform
ranked_gaps.rationale: Massive grassroots demand (r/LocalLLaMA 266K+ members) plus enterprise edge AI growth. Very low competition: no platform covers full edge lifecycle (fine-tune -> quantize -> deploy -> feedback -> retrain). Lower feasibility due to hardware fragmentation and on-device fine-tuning technical challenges.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: End-to-end edge SLM platform: cloud fine-tuning with edge-optimized quantization (GGUF, ONNX, TensorRT), device profiling for target hardware, on-device adaptation via lightweight LoRA updates, telemetry collection for continuous improvement. Support for mobile, IoT, and embedded devices.
ranked_gaps.target_market: IoT companies, mobile app developers, manufacturing, healthcare device makers

ranked_gaps.title: Continuous Learning / Production Feedback Loop (Data Flywheel)
ranked_gaps.rationale: Strong demand as production data is widely recognized as the most valuable fine-tuning signal. Very low competition: OpenPipe captures logs but doesn't close the loop. Amdocs built custom flywheel proving enterprise need. Feasible but requires integrating monitoring, data curation, retraining, and deployment.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Automated data flywheel platform: capture production failures/edge cases -> classify and curate corrective examples -> auto-generate retraining datasets -> trigger fine-tuning runs -> A/B test new model -> deploy if improved -> monitor. Full MLOps integration with human-in-the-loop for quality gates.
ranked_gaps.target_market: Production ML teams, AI-native companies, SaaS companies with AI features

ranked_gaps.title: Compliance-Aware Training Pipeline for Regulated Industries
ranked_gaps.rationale: Domain-specific LLMs are fastest-growing segment (35.1% CAGR) driven by regulated industries. Low competition: Databricks has governance, Lamini has certifications, but no purpose-built compliance-first fine-tuning platform. Moderate feasibility due to complexity of compliance frameworks across industries.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Compliance-first fine-tuning platform with built-in audit trails, data lineage tracking, model provenance, automated compliance reporting (HIPAA, SOX, GDPR), role-based access control for training data, and certifiable training pipelines. On-premise and air-gapped deployment as default.
ranked_gaps.target_market: Healthcare, financial services, legal, government, defense

ranked_gaps.title: Catastrophic Forgetting Prevention and Testing
ranked_gaps.rationale: Universally cited challenge with near-zero competition. Academic research exists (EWC, progressive nets) but no platform implements prevention and testing as a feature. Lower feasibility as the problem is partially unsolved in research.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Integrated module within fine-tuning platforms: automated pre/post fine-tuning benchmark comparison, knowledge retention scoring, selective weight freezing recommendations, and mixed training data recipes that preserve general capabilities while adding domain expertise.
ranked_gaps.target_market: All fine-tuning users, especially those needing general + domain capabilities

ranked_gaps.title: Domain-Adaptive Continued Pretraining as a Service
ranked_gaps.rationale: Research proves CPT is underused and misunderstood. Medium competition: cloud providers offer manual setup. Lower feasibility due to high compute costs and expertise requirements. March 2026 paper 'The Finetuner's Fallacy' validates gap.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Managed CPT service: upload domain corpus, select base model, platform handles tokenization, curriculum design, hyperparameter selection, and training scheduling. Includes cost estimation and expected improvement prediction before committing resources.
ranked_gaps.target_market: Scientific, medical, legal organizations wanting deep domain models

ranked_gaps.title: Cost-Optimized Training Advisor
ranked_gaps.rationale: Strong demand from confused developers and budget-constrained teams. Very low competition: Predibase Fine-tuning Index is closest. Highly feasible using benchmark data and heuristics. Good as feature within existing platforms or standalone advisory tool.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: AI-powered training advisor: describe your task and domain -> get recommendations for base model, training method (LoRA vs full vs CPT), data volume needed, estimated cost, expected quality improvement. Includes benchmark comparisons and ROI calculator.

ranked_gaps.title: Collaborative Domain Expert Annotation and RLHF Platform
ranked_gaps.rationale: Medium demand as RLHF with domain experts is growing. Moderate competition from Labelbox, Kili, Scale AI but these are separate from fine-tuning platforms. Feasible to build as integrated module.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Collaborative annotation interface integrated into fine-tuning platform: domain experts (doctors, lawyers) can review model outputs, provide preference rankings, flag errors, and approve training data. Non-technical UI with domain-specific evaluation rubrics.
ranked_gaps.target_market: Teams working with non-technical subject matter experts

ranked_gaps.title: Multi-Tenant Domain Model Management
ranked_gaps.rationale: Growing demand from SaaS companies and consultancies managing multiple client models. Low competition: Predibase LoRAX handles serving, TrueFoundry has gateway, but no complete management solution. Feasible as extension of existing infrastructure.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Multi-tenant model management platform: per-client LoRA adapters with access control, versioning, A/B testing, cost attribution, usage analytics, and automated model lifecycle management. SaaS-friendly billing and API management.
ranked_gaps.target_market: SaaS companies, consulting firms, managed AI service providers

ranked_gaps.title: Domain Ontology and Terminology Integration
ranked_gaps.rationale: Niche but meaningful demand from domains with formal ontologies (medical, scientific). Nearly zero competition. Lower feasibility as it requires deep integration with tokenization and training pipeline.
ranked_gaps.recommended_solution: Ontology-aware training module: import domain ontologies (SNOMED, ICD, legal citations), use them to guide tokenizer vocabulary expansion, training data generation, and evaluation criteria. Ensures model learns correct relationships between domain concepts.
ranked_gaps.target_market: Medical informatics, scientific research, legal tech
